title: clinopodium nepeta subsp. spruneri
intro: Clinopodium nepeta subsp. spruneri, also known as Calamintha nepeta subsp. spruneri, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mint family Lamiaceae. It is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is known to exhibit antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can provide various health benefits, including relief from digestive issues, respiratory problems, and pain management. The herbal preparations of Clinopodium nepeta subsp. spruneri include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to take advantage of its medicinal and health benefits.
